carry.en: move something from one place to another by holding or supporting it

ex.en: He carried the boxes upstairs.

carry.en: have something with you as you move around

ex.en: She always carries an umbrella in her bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가방에 항상 우산을 가지고 다닌다.

cartoon.en: a funny or satirical drawing, especially in a newspaper or magazine

ex.en: He drew a cartoon about the president.

cartoon.en: a film made by photographing a series of drawings to create the illusion of movement

ex.en: Kids love watching cartoons on Saturday morning.
ex.ko: 아이들은 토요일 아침에 만화영화 보는 것을 좋아한다.

case.en: a particular situation or instance of something

ex.en: In that case, we should leave early.
ex.ko: 그런 경우라면 우리는 일찍 떠나야 한다.

case.en: a matter that is being dealt with by a court of law

ex.en: The lawyer won the case.

case.en: a container designed to hold or carry a particular thing

ex.en: She put her glasses in the case.

cat.en: a small furry animal often kept as a pet

ex.en: The cat is sleeping on the sofa.

catch.en: take hold of something moving through the air

ex.en: He caught the ball with one hand.

catch.en: be in time to get on a bus, train, or plane

ex.en: We need to hurry to catch the train.

catch.en: discover someone doing something wrong, often by surprise

ex.en: She caught him reading her diary.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 자기 일기를 읽는 것을 들켰다.

catch.en: a hidden problem or disadvantage in something that seems good

ex.en: The offer sounds great, but what's the catch?
ex.ko: 그 제안은 좋아 보이지만, 무슨 함정이 있는 거지?

cd.en: a small round disc used for storing music or data

ex.en: I bought a new CD by my favorite singer.
ex.ko: 나는 내가 좋아하는 가수의 새 시디를 샀다.

cd player.en: a machine used to play music from a CD

ex.en: He listens to music on his old CD player.
ex.ko: 그는 오래된 시디 플레이어로 음악을 듣는다.

celebrate.en: do something special to show that an occasion is important

ex.en: We celebrated her birthday with a big party.
ex.ko: 우리는 큰 파티로 그녀의 생일을 축하했다.

celebrate.en: praise something publicly as important or special

ex.en: The film celebrates the courage of ordinary people.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 평범한 사람들의 용기를 기린다.

celebration.en: a special event or party held to celebrate something

ex.en: The wedding celebration lasted all night.
ex.ko: 그 결혼 축하 행사는 밤새 계속되었다.

chair.cue: 앉는 데 쓰는 등받이 있는 것
chair.en: a seat for one person, usually with a back and four legs

ex.en: Please sit on this chair.

chair.en: the person in charge of a meeting or organization

ex.en: She was elected chair of the committee.

change.en: the act or result of becoming different

ex.en: There has been a big change in her attitude.
He changed his plans at the last minute.

change.en: the money returned to you when you pay more than the price, or coins of small value

ex.en: Here is your change.
I need to change before we go out.

character.en: a person in a book, film, or play

ex.en: The main character is a young detective.

character.en: the qualities that make a person who they are

ex.en: She has a strong character.

character.en: a written or printed letter or symbol

ex.en: Chinese characters are difficult to write.

check.en: look at something to make sure it is correct or safe

ex.en: Can you check my homework?

check.en: the bill for food or drinks at a restaurant

ex.en: Could we have the check, please?

check.en: a printed form used to pay money from a bank account

ex.en: He paid the rent by check.

cheese.en: a solid food made from milk

ex.en: She put cheese on the sandwich.

chicken.en: a common farm bird kept for its eggs and meat

ex.en: The farmer keeps chickens in the yard.

chicken.en: the meat of a chicken eaten as food

ex.en: We had fried chicken for dinner.

chicken.en: an informal word for someone who is not brave

ex.en: Don't be a chicken, try the roller coaster!

child.en: a young person who is not yet an adult

ex.en: The child is playing in the garden.

chocolate.en: a sweet brown food made from cacao beans

ex.en: She gave him a box of chocolates.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그에게 초콜릿 한 상자를 주었다.

chocolate.en: a hot drink made from chocolate powder and milk

ex.en: I ordered a hot chocolate to warm up.

choose.en: pick one thing or person from a number of possibilities

ex.en: You can choose any dessert you like.
ex.ko: 네가 좋아하는 디저트를 아무거나 골라도 돼.

church.en: a building used for Christian worship

ex.en: They go to church every Sunday.

cinema.en: a theater where films are shown

ex.en: Let's go to the cinema this weekend.

cinema.en: films generally, considered as an art form

ex.en: She studied French cinema at university.

circle.en: a round shape with no corners

ex.en: Draw a circle on the paper.

circle.en: move around something in a circular path

ex.en: The plane circled the airport before landing.
ex.ko: 그 비행기는 착륙 전에 공항 주위를 선회했다.

circle.en: a group of people who share the same interests or friends

ex.en: She has a small circle of close friends.
ex.ko: 그녀는 친한 친구들의 작은 모임이 있다.

city.en: a large area where many people live and work

ex.en: New York is a huge city.

class.en: put people or things into groups according to shared qualities

ex.en: The wine is classed as a premium variety.

class.en: a group of students who study together, or the lesson they attend

ex.en: Our class starts at nine o'clock.

class.en: a group of people in society who have a similar social or economic position

ex.en: The novel explores differences between social classes.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 사회 계층 간의 차이를 탐구한다.

classmate.en: someone who is in the same class as you at school

ex.en: My classmates helped me with the project.

classroom.en: a room in a school where lessons take place

ex.en: The classroom was very quiet during the test.

clean.en: free from dirt or unwanted marks

ex.en: Keep your room clean.

clean.en: remove dirt or unwanted substances from something

ex.en: I need to clean my room.

clever.en: having a quick and resourceful mind

ex.en: She is a clever student who learns quickly.

climb.en: move upward, especially using your hands and feet

ex.en: They climbed the mountain in five hours.
ex.ko: 그들은 다섯 시간 만에 그 산을 올랐다.

climb.en: an act of climbing, especially a mountain

ex.en: The climb took most of the day.
Temperatures are expected to climb this week.
ex.ko: 이번 주 기온이 상승할 것으로 예상된다.

clock.en: a device that shows the time of day, usually not worn on the body

ex.en: The clock on the wall says three o'clock.
The store is closed on Sundays.

closed.en: not willing to consider new ideas

ex.en: He has a closed mind about new technology.
ex.ko: 그는 새로운 기술에 대해 마음이 닫혀 있다.

cloth.en: material made by weaving or knitting fibers, used to make clothes or other things

ex.en: The dress is made of soft cloth.

clothes.en: items worn to cover the body

ex.en: She hung her clothes in the closet.

cloud.en: a visible mass of water droplets floating in the sky

ex.en: Dark clouds filled the sky before the storm.
ex.ko: 폭풍이 오기 전에 하늘이 먹구름으로 가득 찼다.

cloudy.en: full of or covered with clouds

ex.en: It's cloudy today, so we can't see the sun.
ex.ko: 오늘은 날씨가 흐려서 해를 볼 수 없다.

club.en: a group of people who meet regularly because they share an interest

ex.en: She joined the chess club at school.

club.en: a place where people go at night to dance and drink

ex.en: They danced all night at the club.

club.en: a stick used to hit the ball in golf

ex.en: He bought a new set of golf clubs.

coach.en: train and give instructions to a sports player or team

ex.en: He coaches the school soccer team.

coach.en: a person who trains an athlete or a sports team

ex.en: The coach praised the players after the win.

coach.en: a comfortable bus used for longer journeys

ex.en: We took a coach to London.

coat.cue: 추울 때 겉에 입는 긴 옷
coat.en: an outer garment with sleeves worn over other clothes for warmth

ex.en: Put on your coat before you go outside.

coat.en: a thin layer that covers a surface

ex.en: The wall needs another coat of paint.
ex.ko: 그 벽은 페인트를 한 번 더 칠해야 한다.

code.en: a system of words, letters, or numbers used to send secret messages

ex.en: The spy sent a message in code.

code.en: a set of rules about how to behave

ex.en: Students must follow the school's dress code.
ex.ko: 학생들은 학교의 복장 규정을 따라야 한다.

code.en: the set of instructions that makes up a computer program

ex.en: She spent hours fixing the code.
ex.ko: 그녀는 코드를 고치는 데 몇 시간을 보냈다.

coffee.en: a hot drink made from ground coffee beans

ex.en: He drinks a cup of coffee every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 커피를 한 잔 마신다.

coke.en: a sweet, brown, fizzy soft drink

ex.en: Can I have a coke with ice, please?
ex.ko: 얼음 넣은 콜라 한 잔 주시겠어요?

cold.en: having a low temperature; not warm

ex.en: It's cold outside today.

cold.en: a mild illness that causes a runny nose, cough, and sore throat

ex.en: I have a bad cold this week.
ex.ko: 나는 이번 주 감기가 심하게 걸렸다.

ex.en: He gave her a cold look and walked away.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 차가운 시선을 던지고 가버렸다.

collect.en: gather things together, often as a hobby

ex.en: He collects stamps from different countries.

collect.en: obtain money that is owed or requested

ex.en: The charity collected donations for the flood victims.
ex.ko: 그 자선단체는 홍수 피해자를 위한 기부금을 모았다.

collection.en: a group of things that have been gathered together

ex.en: He has a large collection of old coins.
ex.ko: 그는 오래된 동전을 많이 수집해 놓았다.

college.en: a school for higher education after high school

ex.en: She is studying biology at college.

color.en: the quality that makes things look red, blue, green, etc.

ex.en: What color is your car?

color.en: add color to a picture or object

ex.en: The children colored the picture with crayons.

come.en: move toward the place where the speaker is

ex.en: Come here, please.
The bus comes at nine every morning.

come.en: reach a particular state or condition

ex.en: Her dream finally came true.

computer.en: an electronic machine used to store and process information

ex.en: She works on her computer all day.

concert.en: a musical performance given by singers or musicians

ex.en: We went to a rock concert last night.

contest.en: an event in which people compete to find out who is the best

ex.en: He won first prize in the singing contest.

contest.en: formally state that you disagree with or oppose a decision

ex.en: She decided to contest the decision in court.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 결정에 대해 법정에서 이의를 제기하기로 했다.

ex.en: We had a long conversation about our plans.
ex.ko: 우리는 계획에 대해 긴 대화를 나눴다.

ex.en: She is cooking dinner for the family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가족을 위해 저녁을 요리하고 있다.

cook.en: a person whose job is to prepare and cook food

ex.en: He works as a cook at a small restaurant.
She baked chocolate chip cookies.

cookie.en: a small piece of data that a website stores on your computer

ex.en: This site uses cookies to remember your settings.
ex.ko: 이 사이트는 설정을 기억하기 위해 쿠키를 사용한다.

cop.en: an informal word for a police officer

ex.en: The cop stopped the car for speeding.

copy.en: make an exact copy of something

ex.en: Please copy this page for me.

copy.en: a thing made to be exactly like another thing

ex.en: Keep a copy of the document.

corn.en: a tall plant with yellow kernels, grown for food

ex.en: We grilled fresh corn for the barbecue.
ex.ko: 우리는 바비큐를 위해 신선한 옥수수를 구웠다.

corner.en: the place where two streets meet

ex.en: Turn left at the corner.

corner.en: the place where two walls or edges meet inside a room or object

ex.en: The lamp is in the corner of the room.

correct.en: make something right after it was wrong

ex.en: The teacher corrected our mistakes.

correct.en: free from error; true or accurate

ex.en: All of your answers were correct.

couch.en: a comfortable seat for more than one person

ex.en: He fell asleep on the couch.

could.en: used as the past tense of 'can' to talk about past ability

ex.en: When I was young, I could run very fast.
ex.ko: 어렸을 때 나는 아주 빨리 달릴 수 있었다.

could.en: used to politely ask someone to do something

ex.en: Could you close the window, please?

course.en: a series of lessons on a particular subject

ex.en: She is taking a course in French.

course.en: the way something develops or progresses over time

ex.en: Things changed during the course of the year.

course.en: one part of a meal served separately

ex.en: We had a three-course dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 세 가지 코스로 된 저녁을 먹었다.

cousin.en: the child of your aunt or uncle

ex.en: I'm visiting my cousin this weekend.
ex.ko: 나는 이번 주말에 사촌을 만나러 간다.

cover.en: put something over another thing to protect or hide it

ex.en: She covered the baby with a blanket.

cover.en: the outer covering of a book or magazine

ex.en: The book has a beautiful cover.

cover.en: include or deal with a particular subject

ex.en: The course covers basic grammar.

cow.en: a large farm animal kept for milk

ex.en: The cow gives milk every morning.
ex.ko: 그 소는 매일 아침 우유를 준다.

cream.en: the thick, fatty part of milk

ex.en: She added cream to her coffee.

cream.en: a smooth substance applied to the skin

ex.en: He put cream on his hands.
She wore a cream dress to the wedding.

credit card.en: a plastic card used to buy things and pay for them later

ex.en: He paid for the meal with a credit card.

cry.en: shed tears because you are sad or hurt

ex.en: The baby cried all night.
"Look out!" she cried.

cry.en: a loud sound made by a person or animal

ex.en: They heard a cry for help.

culture.en: the customs, beliefs, and way of life shared by a group of people

ex.en: She is interested in Korean culture.

cup.cue: 마실 때 쓰는 손잡이 있는 그릇
cup.en: a small container with a handle, used for drinking

ex.en: He drank a cup of tea.

cup.en: a unit used to measure an amount of food in cooking

ex.en: Add two cups of flour.

cut.en: divide something into pieces with a sharp tool

ex.en: She cut the paper with scissors.

cut.en: a wound made by something sharp

ex.en: He has a cut on his finger.

cut.en: reduce the amount, size, or number of something

ex.en: The company cut prices by ten percent.

cute.en: attractive, especially because of being small or pretty

ex.en: That puppy is so cute!
My dad picks me up from school.

daddy.en: a child's informal word for father

ex.en: The little girl ran to her daddy.

dance.en: move your body in time to music

ex.en: They danced all night at the wedding.

dance.en: a series of movements performed to music

ex.en: She learned a new dance for the show.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공연을 위해 새로운 춤을 배웠다.

dancing.en: the activity of moving your body in time to music

ex.en: Dancing is her favorite hobby.
The room was too dark to read in.
ex.ko: 그 방은 너무 어두워서 책을 읽을 수 없었다.

dark.en: (of a color) deep and strong rather than pale

ex.en: She wore a dark blue jacket.

date.en: a particular day of the month or year

ex.en: What's today's date?

date.en: go out with someone you are romantically interested in

ex.en: They have been dating for two years.

date.en: a romantic meeting between two people

ex.en: He asked her out on a date.
They have two sons and one daughter.
ex.ko: 그들은 아들 둘과 딸 하나가 있다.

ex.en: I run every day.

day.en: the time when it is light outside, between sunrise and sunset

ex.en: We worked all day and rested at night.
ex.ko: 우리는 낮 동안 일하고 밤에 쉬었다.

dear.en: a word used to affectionately address someone, especially at the start of a letter

ex.en: Dear John, how are you?
She is a dear friend of mine.

dear.en: costing a lot of money (British usage)

ex.en: Petrol has become very dear this year.

december.en: the twelfth and last month of the year

ex.en: Christmas is in December.
This soup is delicious.

describe.en: say what someone or something is like

ex.en: Can you describe the man you saw?
ex.ko: 네가 본 남자를 묘사해 줄 수 있어?

design.en: plan and make a drawing of how something will look or work

ex.en: She designed the new company logo.

design.en: the way something is planned to look or work

ex.en: I love the design of this chair.
ex.ko: 나는 이 의자의 디자인이 정말 마음에 든다.

desk.en: a piece of furniture with a flat top used for writing or working

ex.en: He sat at his desk and started working.

dictionary.en: a book that lists words and explains what they mean

ex.en: She looked up the word in a dictionary.

did.en: the past tense of 'do', used to talk about past actions or to form questions and negatives

ex.en: She did her homework yesterday.

difference.en: the way in which two things are not the same

ex.en: There is a big difference between the two plans.
ex.ko: 두 계획 사이에는 큰 차이가 있다.

ex.en: My opinion is different from yours.

difficult.en: not easy to do or understand

ex.en: This math problem is very difficult.

dig.en: make a hole in the ground using a tool or your hands

ex.en: The dog is digging a hole in the yard.
ex.ko: 그 개는 마당에 구덩이를 파고 있다.

dining room.en: a room in a house where meals are eaten

ex.en: We eat dinner together in the dining room.

dinner.cue: 하루 중 저녁에 먹는 주된 식사
dinner.en: the main meal of the day, usually eaten in the evening

ex.en: We had dinner at seven o'clock.

dirty.en: covered with dirt or unwanted marks

ex.en: His shoes were dirty after the hike.

discuss.en: talk about a subject with other people

ex.en: We discussed the problem for an hour.
ex.ko: 우리는 한 시간 동안 그 문제를 논의했다.

dish.en: a flat container used for holding or serving food

ex.en: Please wash the dishes after dinner.
ex.ko: 저녁 식사 후 접시를 씻어 주세요.

dish.en: a particular type of prepared food

ex.en: This is my favorite dish at the restaurant.
ex.ko: 이건 이 식당에서 내가 가장 좋아하는 요리다.

ex.en: What are you doing this weekend?

do.en: used with another verb to form questions and negative sentences

ex.en: Do you like coffee?

doctor.en: a person who is trained to treat sick or injured people

ex.en: She went to see the doctor about her cough.

does.en: the third person singular present tense of 'do'

ex.en: She does her homework every day.

dog.en: a common animal often kept as a pet, known for barking

ex.en: The dog ran after the ball.

doing.cue: do의 현재분사형 — 지금 하고 있는 행동
doing.en: the present participle of 'do', used to talk about an action in progress

ex.en: What are you doing right now?

doll.en: a small toy shaped like a person

ex.en: The little girl played with her doll.
ex.ko: 그 어린 소녀는 인형을 가지고 놀았다.

dollar.en: the unit of money used in the United States and some other countries

ex.en: The book costs ten dollars.

door.en: a barrier that opens and closes to let people enter or leave a room or building

ex.en: Please close the door behind you.

down.en: toward a lower place or position

ex.en: Please sit down.
He's been feeling a bit down lately.
ex.ko: 그는 요즘 기분이 좀 안 좋다.

drama.en: a story performed by actors on TV, in a movie, or on stage

ex.en: She loves watching Korean dramas.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한국 드라마 보는 것을 좋아한다.

drama.en: an exciting or emotional event or situation

ex.en: There was a lot of drama at the party last night.

dream.en: a series of images and feelings that happen in your mind while you sleep

ex.en: I had a strange dream last night.
I dreamed about flying last night.

dream.en: something you hope to achieve or have

ex.en: Her dream is to become a doctor.
She dressed quickly and left the house.
ex.ko: 그녀는 재빨리 옷을 입고 집을 나섰다.

dress.en: a piece of clothing for women or girls that covers the body and part of the legs

ex.en: She wore a beautiful red dress.

drink.en: take liquid into your mouth and swallow it

ex.en: He drank a glass of water.
Would you like a drink?
He doesn't drink or smoke.
She has a lot of drive and determination.

drive.en: operate and control a car or other vehicle

ex.en: He drives to work every day.
We went for a drive along the coast.

driver.en: a person who drives a vehicle

ex.en: The bus driver stopped at every station.
ex.ko: 그 버스 운전자는 모든 정류장에 정차했다.

drop.en: let something fall, often by accident

ex.en: She dropped her phone on the floor.

drop.en: a small amount of liquid that falls in a round shape

ex.en: A drop of rain fell on my hand.

drop.en: decrease suddenly in amount or level

ex.en: Temperatures dropped sharply overnight.

drum.en: a musical instrument that you hit to make a sound

ex.en: He plays the drums in a band.

due.en: required to be paid or done by a certain time

ex.en: The rent is due on the first of the month.
The train is due to arrive at noon.

dvd.en: a disc used to store and play movies or data

ex.en: We watched a DVD at home last night.

each.en: every one of two or more things, considered separately

ex.en: Each student got a book.
ex.ko: 각 학생은 책을 한 권씩 받았다.

each.en: to or for every one, considered separately

ex.en: The tickets cost ten dollars each.

each other.en: used to say that two or more people do the same thing to or for the other

ex.en: They love each other very much.

ear.en: the body part used for hearing

ex.en: She whispered something in his ear.

early.en: before the usual or expected time

ex.en: I woke up early this morning.

early.en: near the beginning of a period of time

ex.en: She arrived in the early morning.

easy.en: not difficult to do or understand

ex.en: The test was easy for her.

eat.en: put food in your mouth and swallow it

ex.en: We ate lunch together.

egg.en: an oval object laid by a hen, eaten as food

ex.en: She fried two eggs for breakfast.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아침으로 달걀 두 개를 부쳤다.

ex.en: She has eight books on her desk.
ex.ko: 그녀는 책상에 책 여덟 권을 가지고 있다.

ex.en: My brother turned eighteen last week.
ex.ko: 내 남동생은 지난주에 열여덟 살이 되었다.

ex.en: My grandmother is eighty years old.

either.en: one or the other of two things or people

ex.en: You can choose either color.
ex.ko: 둘 중 아무 색이나 골라도 된다.

either.en: used after a negative statement to mean 'also'

ex.en: I don't like coffee either.
This is an elementary math problem.
My daughter goes to elementary school.
There are eleven players on a soccer team.

else.en: other than the person, thing, or place already mentioned

ex.en: Is there anything else you need?

email.en: send a message to someone using the internet

ex.en: I'll email you the report tonight.

email.en: a message sent electronically over the internet

ex.en: She checks her email every morning.
We watched the movie until the end.

end.en: come or bring to a finish

ex.en: The concert ended at ten o'clock.

engineer.en: skillfully plan or bring about a complex event or situation

ex.en: They engineered a surprise reunion for their parents.
ex.ko: 그들은 부모님을 위한 깜짝 재회를 계획해냈다.

engineer.en: a person who designs or builds machines, structures, or systems

ex.en: He works as a software engineer.

enjoy.en: get pleasure from doing or experiencing something

ex.en: We enjoyed the concert last night.

evening.cue: 해가 진 후 밤이 되기 전
evening.en: the part of the day between afternoon and night

ex.en: We usually have dinner in the evening.
The school is holding a sports event next week.
ex.ko: 학교는 다음 주에 스포츠 행사를 개최한다.

event.en: something that happens, especially something important

ex.en: The wedding was the biggest event of the year.
ex.ko: 그 결혼식은 그 해의 가장 큰 행사였다.

every.en: used to refer to all the members of a group without exception

ex.en: Every student passed the test.

everybody.cue: 모든 사람 한 명 한 명

ex.en: Everybody enjoyed the party.

everyday.en: happening or used every day; ordinary

ex.en: Cooking is part of my everyday routine.

everyone.cue: 모든 사람 한 명 한 명

ex.en: Everyone was happy with the result.
She lost everything in the fire.
I looked everywhere for my keys.
ex.ko: 나는 열쇠를 찾으려고 어디든 다 뒤졌다.

example.en: something that shows what a general idea or rule is like

ex.en: Can you give me an example?
ex.ko: 예를 하나 들어 줄 수 있어?

ex.en: She did an excellent job on the project.

excited.en: feeling happy and full of energy because something good is happening

ex.en: The kids were excited about the trip.

exciting.en: causing feelings of excitement and interest

ex.en: It was an exciting football match.

excuse.en: a reason you give to explain why you did something wrong

ex.en: He gave a poor excuse for being late.
ex.ko: 그는 지각한 것에 대해 궁색한 변명을 했다.

excuse.en: forgive someone for a small mistake

ex.en: Please excuse my mistake.

excuse.cue: 정중하게 말을 걸거나 양해를 구할 때
excuse.en: used to politely get someone's attention or apologize for interrupting

ex.en: Excuse me, could you help me?

exercise.en: physical activity done to stay healthy and fit

ex.en: Regular exercise is good for you.

exercise.en: do physical activity to keep your body healthy

ex.en: She exercises every morning.

exercise.en: a task done to practice a skill

ex.en: Complete the exercises at the end of the chapter.
That restaurant is too expensive for us.

eye.en: the body part used for seeing

ex.en: She has beautiful blue eyes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아름다운 파란 눈을 가지고 있다.

face.en: the front part of the head, from the forehead to the chin

ex.en: She has a kind face.
ex.ko: 그녀는 인상이 좋은 얼굴을 가지고 있다.

face.en: deal with a difficult or unpleasant situation directly

ex.en: We have to face our problems.

factory.en: a building where goods are manufactured

ex.en: His father works at a car factory.
